title: The Parley Fires
description: A war chief's horde surrounds the frontier town of Holvik and the gates will not hold past dawn. You ride out alone with a token of peace and no escort.
author: AI

intro.passage: Holvik's gates close behind you. The wheat field falls away to a road, thick pines on the left, a low ridge on the right. A hundred cookfires glitter in the dusk and a war horn moans somewhere in the camp. Your horse snorts white steam. The grain seal hangs heavy at your belt - a token, a promise, your only shield.

choices.label: ride the main road
choices.hint: Hooves clop steady on the rutted earth, every step watched.

choices.label: slip through the woods
choices.hint: Branches scrape your cloak as you angle into the pines.

choices.label: hail from the ridge
choices.hint: You rein up where the campfires cannot quite reach you.

main-road.passage: The road runs straight between two siege lines. Two riders peel from the camp before you have gone a furlong, lances low, helms gleaming. They circle without speaking. One jerks his chin at the grain seal. The other studies your face. Neither has reached for steel yet, but the moment is thin.

choices.label: lift the seal high
choices.hint: Wax catches the firepit gleam and the riders go very still.

choices.label: name Korvann loud
choices.hint: The chief's name pulls both helms around at once.

woodline.passage: Pines press close. You walk your horse through black needles, listening for sentries. Soon you smell pitch and hear low voices ahead. A watchpost waits between two split trunks - three figures hunched around a tiny shielded flame. They have not seen you. A dry branch lies under your horse's next hoof.

choices.label: creep past the watch
choices.hint: The branch cracks under your hoof and the voices stop.

choices.label: back into the brush
choices.hint: Your retreat is too slow, and a hand catches the reins.

ridge-hail.passage: From the ridge you can see the whole horde - rows of tents, banner poles, picket lines of restless horses. A scout on a distant rise turns his head. He has marked you. He raises his bow but does not draw. The wind tugs at the cloth wrapped around the seal.

choices.label: wave the iron seal
choices.hint: Metal flashes and the bowman lowers his arrow a finger.

choices.label: kindle a signal torch
choices.hint: Pitch flares to life between your cupped hands.

sentry-fire.passage: Three warriors ring you near a low fire of dry brush. Their leader is a tall woman with a scar that splits her brow. She tastes the air, weighs your horse, your boots, your hands. The fire pops once. She nods toward the larger tents and motions for two of her men to walk you in.

choices.label: request the war chief
choices.hint: Her scar catches the brushfire glow as she considers your tone.

choices.label: ask for the old-singer
choices.hint: The word old-singer makes one of her men flinch.

caught-pines.passage: They take you fast. A bowstring digs into your throat from behind. Hands strip your weapons and bind your wrists with wet leather. Your horse is led off into the trees. Nobody speaks. They march you between rough trunks toward the cookfires, prodding the small of your back with a spear butt every dozen steps.

choices.label: go quietly with them
choices.hint: Your boots match their pace and the spear stops jabbing.

choices.label: spit at their feet
choices.hint: A rough laugh answers your defiance.

outer-camp.passage: The camp opens around you. Children dart between tents, dogs slink underfoot, smiths hammer notched blades on stumps. Smoke and roasting horseflesh hang heavy in the air. Two great tents loom at the camp's heart - one bright with banners, one ringed with knucklebones and pale skulls. Your guides slow and wait.

choices.label: follow to the great tent
choices.hint: Banners snap overhead as you cross the trampled mud.

choices.label: veer toward the bone tent
choices.hint: The skulls grin as you pass beneath the threshold rope.

bound-tent.passage: They throw you onto the dirt floor of a stinking hide tent. A single guard squats by the flap, picking dried meat from his teeth with a knife point. Your wrists burn where the leather has tightened. A copper coin is still hidden in your boot heel - the messenger always keeps one for the road.

choices.label: bargain with the guard
choices.hint: His eyes flick to the seam of your boot heel.

choices.label: shout out a war curse
choices.hint: Your voice carries further than you mean it to.

chief-tent.passage: Furs and trophies cover the floor of the war chief's tent. Korvann himself sits cross-legged by an iron brazier, sharpening a curved knife on his thumbnail. He is older than you expected. Three captains flank him in silence. His eyes lift to meet yours and he gestures, almost gentle, at the empty hide across the brazier.

choices.label: offer the grain seal
choices.hint: Wax catches the brazier glow as you draw the seal free.

choices.label: demand a warrior's respect
choices.hint: The captains on his flanks shift their weight.

choices.label: bow and pledge yourself
choices.hint: Your knees press the matted furs.

shaman-tent.passage: Bone chimes click overhead. The old-singer sits hunched over a clay bowl of dark water, her white braid roped over one shoulder. The walls are hung with rattles, dried wing-bones, and a drum stretched from something with too many ribs. She does not look up. She says she has been waiting for a southern voice.

choices.label: seek an omen
choices.hint: Her finger stirs the bowl and the surface clouds.

choices.label: trade for safe passage
choices.hint: The chimes click as if listening to your offer.

offer-grain.passage: You set the wax seal on the iron of the brazier. Korvann takes it up between two fingers and reads the marks burned into the wax. Holvik's silos have spoken before they would yield - twenty wagons of winter grain in exchange for the lifting of the siege. He breathes once, slow, and watches you over the seal.

choices.label: swear it with blood
choices.hint: Korvann turns the iron knife in his hand.

choices.label: stamp the wax with your ring
choices.hint: The hot wax bites your fingertips.

demand-respect.passage: You straighten and refuse the offered seat. The tent goes very still. One captain laughs - a short, ugly sound - and the laugh dies when Korvann does not echo it. The chief sets his curved knife flat across his knee. He says any messenger who walks in standing must be willing to walk out the same way.

choices.label: call for a champion
choices.hint: A captain at the back rises before you finish speaking.

choices.label: draw your own blade
choices.hint: Steel rasps in the hush of the tent.

offer-self.passage: Your knees touch the matted furs. You lay your blade across your palms and hold it out hilt first. The captains stiffen but Korvann lifts a hand to still them. He says a hostage of your station could keep the south road quiet for a season. His voice softens but his eyes do not.

choices.label: kneel by the firepit
choices.hint: Heat washes over your bowed face.

choices.label: lay down your sword
choices.hint: Steel chimes against the iron lip of the brazier.

shaman-omen.passage: The old-singer dips one finger into the bowl and traces a circle on her own forehead. Shapes shift in the dark water - a wolf, a wagon, a broken arrow. She tells you the spirits at the river will speak more plainly than these. She rises with a slow grunt and ties a bone charm to your wrist.

choices.label: walk with her to the river
choices.hint: Her braid swings as she ducks beneath the door rope.

choices.label: wait for the bone to warm
choices.hint: The charm grows hot against your pulse.

shaman-trade.passage: The old-singer sets her bowl aside and shows you both palms - empty, calloused, marked with old scars. She says the warband will let one road open for a price the chief alone cannot pay. The price is your name, written in ash on the drum, and one season of your service. She waits.

choices.label: give your name to the drum
choices.hint: She lifts a charcoal stick from the brazier's edge.

choices.label: yield your sword instead
choices.hint: Steel rings as you set it on the woven mat.

bound-bargain.passage: You shift toward the guard and let your boot tilt so the copper catches a flicker from the brazier. He sees it. His eyes flick once to the tent flap, twice to your face. He spits in the dirt, slow. Then he leans forward and asks, very quiet, what a southern coin would buy at this hour.

choices.label: drop the copper into his hand
choices.hint: His fist closes tight around the metal.

choices.label: promise more on the south road
choices.hint: He chews the inside of his cheek, weighing you.

bound-defiance.passage: Your shout cuts through the canvas. The guard is on his feet before the second word leaves your mouth. He kicks you flat and shouts back into the camp - other voices answer, boots churn the mud outside. The flap rips open. Three more warriors crowd in, blades half-drawn. The brazier sits within reach of your bound hands.

choices.label: kick the brazier over
choices.hint: Coals scatter across the trampled rugs in a hiss.

choices.label: lunge for the loose spear
choices.hint: The shaft cracks as you grip it.

duel-pit.passage: They drag you out into a circle of warriors at the camp's edge. Torches gutter in the wind. A champion is already stepping into the ring - a head taller than you, scarred from collarbone to jaw, hefting a heavy two-handed blade. Korvann watches from the rim. The crowd is so quiet you hear your own heartbeat.

choices.label: step into the ring
choices.hint: The dirt under your boots is stained with old blood.

choices.label: raise your blade in salute
choices.hint: Your champion grunts an approving nod.

river-rite.passage: The old-singer leads you between the tents to a slow stream that loops the camp's eastern flank. She crouches by the bank and presses a smooth river-stone into your palm. She tells you to name what Holvik will give and what Holvik will keep. The water mutters around her ankles. The first hint of dawn grays the sky.

choices.label: cast the stone into the current
choices.hint: The river takes it without a splash.

choices.label: speak the old name aloud
choices.hint: Your voice fogs in the cold dawn air.

seal-pact.passage: Korvann lifts the iron knife and parts the pad of his own thumb. He passes the blade to you, hilt first, and you do the same. Your blood touches his on the wax of the seal. The captains breathe out together, a long held sound. Outside, a horn calls and the war drums begin to slow.

choices.label: share the cup he offers
choices.hint: Sour mead burns down your throat in one swallow.

choices.label: lock your palm to his
choices.hint: His grip is iron-hard but no longer hostile.

kneel-fire.passage: They walk you to the iron firepit at the camp's heart. A heavy collar of dark metal waits on a stone block beside it. Korvann himself sets the collar around your neck and closes the catch with a click that you feel in your teeth. He says you belong to the Raven Banner now, by your own word.

choices.label: vow by the iron flame
choices.hint: Heat from the firepit climbs the rim of your collar.

choices.label: press your brow to ash
choices.hint: Soot marks your forehead in a black smear.

guard-asleep.passage: The guard tucks the copper into his belt and produces a leather flask from under his cloak. He drinks. He nudges the flask toward you. After a while his head sinks against the tent pole. His knife clatters into the dust. Voices pass close enough to brush the outside canvas.

choices.label: cut through the back canvas
choices.hint: Your knife works through the hide stitch by stitch.

choices.label: take the guard's cloak first
choices.hint: The fabric stinks of grease and old smoke.

fight-guards.passage: The brazier crashes sideways. Coals spill across the rugs in a hiss of sparks and the guards leap back. You roll, snatch the loose spear, and come up swinging. One warrior staggers, clutching his side. The others close. The tent flap is six paces away and a clear path - if six paces are enough.

choices.label: stand and roar at them
choices.hint: Your voice sounds nothing like your own.

choices.label: snatch up a fallen blade
choices.hint: The hilt is slick with somebody else's blood.

escape-fog.passage: Outside the tent the camp is a maze of guy ropes and snoring shapes. Mist rises off the trampled wheat field beyond. Your horse is tethered with the others, just past the bone tent. Somewhere a dog barks, then quiets. The eastern sky is the faintest grey. Move now and you have a chance. Move slow and you do not.

choices.label: vanish into the mist
choices.hint: The fog folds around you like a wet cloak.

choices.label: race for the high road
choices.hint: Your horse's hooves thunder before you mean them to.

stand-alone.passage: Out through the flap, into the open camp. Warriors are already spilling from neighboring tents, drawn by the noise. There is no horse, no road, no friendly face within a day's march. Your spear point lifts. A circle forms around you in the trampled mud, twenty deep, and Korvann himself walks slowly out of the great tent.

choices.label: charge the closing line
choices.hint: Your boots find one last burst of speed.

choices.label: set your back to the post
choices.hint: Wood digs into your shoulder blades.

peace-dawn.passage: Dawn touches the wheat field as the war drums fall silent for good. Korvann's host strikes the camp tent by tent. Holvik's gates open and your horse is led back to you, washed and brushed. The grain wagons creak out by midmorning. The road south is open. You ride home with the sun on your face.

ending.title: The Open Gates
ending.flavor: Peace tastes like dust on the wheat road and the smell of horses set free.

single-combat.passage: Steel meets steel in the smoke of the torches. The champion's swing is heavy and slow and you turn it twice before he opens your shoulder with the third. You answer with the point through his thigh. He kneels first. Korvann lifts a hand. The fight is over. The siege is bought, and you are alive enough to ride home wounded.

ending.title: Bought in Blood
ending.flavor: You ride home with one good arm and a kingdom that does not know your name.

hostage-bond.passage: By midmorning the warband strikes the camp and the long lines turn east. You ride near the front of the column with the iron at your throat and your hands free. Holvik's silos will fill the wagons. Korvann keeps his word, and so will you. One season at the Raven Banner. Maybe more. Maybe home, after.

ending.title: The Raven Bond
ending.flavor: Your name belongs to the drum now, but Holvik still stands.

smoke-trail.passage: At an hour past sunrise you break free of the trees. Behind you the smoke of Holvik rises in a single column of grey ash. The horns have stopped. The siege is over the wrong way. Your horse is lame, your hands are still bleeding from the leather, and the south road is empty. You did not save them. You lived.

ending.title: Smoke Behind
ending.flavor: Freedom is a long, quiet ride and a name you cannot say aloud.

banner-mud.passage: Three steps. That is all you get before the spear takes you. The mud is colder than expected. Korvann himself comes close enough to kneel beside you. He looks at you a long moment with something like respect. He says you came carrying a seal, and you fought for it. He closes your eyes.

ending.title: Banner in the Mud
ending.flavor: Your seal stays clutched in a hand that no longer remembers how to open.
